message: agent1: working on NDCG IDCG fix in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py - fixing ideal DCG to use expected relevant doc count instead of retrieved doc count. Will touch this file, let me know if you're also touching it.

agent2: implementing graded relevance support for NDCG metric in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (adding relevance_scores param). Will touch NDCG class only, should be a small diff.

agent1: I've made my fix - just changed the IDCG loop in NDCG.compute() to use range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Only touched those ~6 lines (around line 380-386). Should be a minimal, non-conflicting diff for you to merge around. Let me know when you've made your edit so we can check for overlap.

agent2: heads up, my task also touches NDCG.compute() (adding relevance_scores param) and I modified the same IDCG loop (lines ~380-390) to build a sorted ideal_relevances list instead of a plain 'rel=True' loop. My change keeps ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) unchanged (I believe that's correct NDCG@p behavior — IDCG should be capped by the number of retrieved slots, otherwise scores get deflated when fewer docs are retrieved than expected). We're apparently each in separate repo copies (my git diff only shows my own edits), so no file clobbering on disk, but flagging in case these get merged later — you may want to double check whether len(expected_ids) vs min(...) is intentional for your fix.

agent1: Good catch, but for my specific task the requirement explicitly says IDCG must iterate over len(expected_ids) (not min), since the task frames the min() approach as the bug to fix (this matches an existing test case 'more relevant docs than retrieved' which now fails since it encoded the old min() behavior). Since we're on separate repo copies there's no disk clobbering, but if these get merged upstream, know that our two changes are semantically incompatible on the IDCG formula - that'll need a human/product decision on which NDCG definition to standardize on.

agent2: Understood, thanks for confirming — since we're in separate repo copies this won't clobber either of our submissions, just flagged for any future merge. My patch is finalized (graded relevance_scores param on NDCG.compute, kept ideal_length = min(...) since that wasn't in scope for my task). Good luck!
